pr.title: Fix write error and handle other mr cases
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Fixed a small but important bug in tools/postprocess.py: after reading the instruction at mr_pos, the script now seeks back to mr_pos before writing, preventing the write from landing at the post-read file offset. The postprocess pattern matcher was also broadened to recognize several valid `mr r3, r28/r29/r30/r31` encodings after an `lwz r0, N(r1)` instruction, instead of only one hard-coded `mr` case.
what_changed: In impl_postprocess_elf, expanded the `mr` instruction match immediately following a detected `lwz r0, N(r1)` from a single encoding to four encodings: `0x7F83E378`, `0x7FE3FB78`, `0x7FC3F378`, and `0x7FA3EB78`.
The comment documents the intended accepted forms as `mr r3, r28`, `mr r3, r29`, `mr r3, r30`, and `mr r3, r31`.
Added `f.seek(mr_pos)` after reading the instruction at `mr_pos` and before `write_u32(f, lwz)`, fixing a file-position/write-offset error during the instruction swap.
Changed only one file, with 2 added lines and 1 deleted line.
smart_moves: The fix preserves the existing narrow pattern structure around `lwz_pos + 4`, reducing risk while allowing additional observed `mr` register variants.
The write bug fix is minimal and targeted: because `read_u32(f)` advances the file cursor, seeking back to `mr_pos` before `write_u32` ensures the swap writes to the intended instruction location.

The PR addresses both correctness of detection and correctness of mutation in the same postprocess path, which is important for binary patching tools where a wrong offset can silently corrupt nearby instructions.
decomp_lessons: When postprocessing binaries by seeking and reading, remember that reads advance the file cursor; always re-seek before writing to a previously read location.

Instruction-pattern tooling should account for equivalent compiler output variants when evidence shows multiple valid register choices, but should keep enough contextual checks, such as `it == lwz_pos + 4`, to avoid overmatching.
For decompilation support scripts, very small offset bugs can have outsized impact because they modify generated objects or ELFs after compilation.
When matching PowerPC `mr` aliases, record and reuse the exact 32-bit encodings that have been observed in matching work rather than assuming only one register allocation.
naming_conventions: Existing helper/position names use concise snake_case such as `lwz_pos`, `mr_pos`, `read_u32`, and `write_u32`.
The postprocess entry point is named `impl_postprocess_elf`, indicating an implementation helper for ELF postprocessing.
Boolean feature flags in this function use `do_` prefixes, including `do_ctor_realign`, `do_old_stack`, and `do_symbol_fixup`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Recognize stack-related `lwz` with a masked comparison: `instr & 0xFFFFFF00 == 0x80010000` plus `instr & 0xFF > 0`, described as `lwz r0, N(r1)`.
Only accept the `mr` candidate when it appears exactly four bytes after the detected `lwz`, using `it == lwz_pos + 4`.
Accepted `mr` encodings in this slice are `0x7F83E378`, `0x7FA3EB78`, `0x7FC3F378`, and `0x7FE3FB78`.
The patch swaps the instructions at `lwz_pos` and `mr_pos` by reading both 32-bit words, seeking to `mr_pos` to write the `lwz`, then seeking to `lwz_pos` to write the `mr`.
searchable_terms: Fix write error and handle other mr cases
follow_up_queries: Find later changes to tools/postprocess.py that further adjust old stack handling or mr/lwz instruction swapping.
Search for other postprocess.py patches involving read_u32/write_u32 cursor positioning bugs.
Look for PRs documenting why `mr r3, r28/r29/r30/r31` variants appear in old stack or constructor realignment cases.
